Travel Guide 2025

Seoul & Jeju Island

🌱 Vegetarian Friendly 🗺️ Google Maps for Every Spot 🚇 Transit Tips 📍 COEX Deep Dive 🏝️ Jeju Island 🗣️ Key Phrases
Phrases Arrival Transit COEX Han River Bukchon Daejeon Jeju
📍 Note: Google Maps pins in this guide may not be perfectly accurate — always double-check the location on Naver Map or Kakao Map before heading out.
01

Essential Phrases 필수 한국어

🌱 For the Vegetarian
I am vegetarian and do not eat meat
저는 채식주의자이고 고기를 먹지 않습니다
Jeo-neun chae-sik-ju-ui-ja-i-go go-gi-reul meok-ji an-seub-ni-da
No meat please
고기 없이 주세요
Go-gi eob-si ju-se-yo
Does this contain meat?
이 음식에 고기가 들어가나요?
I eum-sik-e go-gi-ga deu-reo-ga-na-yo?
Does this have seafood or fish?
해산물이나 생선이 들어가나요?
Hae-san-mul-i-na saeng-seon-i deu-reo-ga-na-yo?
Is there a vegetarian option?
채식 메뉴가 있나요?
Chae-sik me-nyu-ga it-na-yo?
No anchovies in the broth
육수에도 멸치 없이 해주세요
Yuk-su-e-do myeol-chi eob-si hae-ju-se-yo
Vegetarian version please
채식으로 해주세요
Chae-sik-eu-ro hae-ju-se-yo
🚻 Getting Around & Basics
Where is the bathroom?
화장실이 어디에 있나요?
Hwa-jang-sil-i eo-di-e it-na-yo?
May I use your bathroom?
화장실을 써도 될까요?
Hwa-jang-sil-eul sseo-do doel-kka-yo?
Excuse me / Hey!
저기요!
Jeo-gi-yo!
How much is this?
이거 얼마예요?
I-geo eol-ma-ye-yo?
Can I pay by card?
카드로 결제 돼요?
Ka-deu-ro gyeol-je dwae-yo?
One more please
하나 더 주세요
Ha-na deo ju-se-yo
This is delicious!
너무 맛있어요!
Neo-mu ma-si-sseo-yo!
English menu?
영어 메뉴 있나요?
Yeong-eo me-nyu it-na-yo?
I'd like to order
주문할게요
Ju-mun-hal-ge-yo
Thank you
감사합니다
Gam-sa-ham-ni-da
⚠️ Vegetarian warning: Korean cooking often uses anchovy broth (멸치육수) and fermented shrimp (새우젓) in kimchi and side dishes — even "vegetable" dishes. Always ask specifically. Showing the Korean text on your phone works excellently in most restaurants.
02

Getting from the Airport 공항 → 시내

AREX Express Train Fastest

Non-stop Incheon Airport → Seoul Station in 43 min. Runs every 30 min, 5:20am–11pm. Cost: ~₩9,500 (~$7). Buy at airport station or via T-money.

AREX All-Stop Budget

Stops at Hongdae, Digital Media City, etc. Takes ~56 min. Only ~₩4,150. Best if your hotel is near a stop.

Airport Bus 6103 To COEX

Direct to Gangnam/COEX area. 70–90 min depending on traffic. ~₩18,000. Buy at numbered counters outside arrivals hall.

KakaoTaxi Door to Door

~₩60,000–80,000 to central Seoul. Download KakaoTaxi app (English). Black "deluxe" taxis cost more but are nicer. Join the official queue outside arrivals.

T-Money Card & Connectivity

📱 T-money Card Needs Cash to Buy

교통카드 — Works on all subways, buses, and some taxis

Buy at any convenience store (GS25, CU, 7-Eleven, Emart24) — they are on literally every corner. Card costs ₩3,000–₩4,000 in cash (card purchase is cash-only). Recharge at subway machines or convenience stores with cash or card. Tap in and out on every subway and bus ride.

💡 Cash tip: Bring some Korean Won from home or exchange at the airport. You'll need cash for the T-money card, some street food stalls, and temples. ATMs at GS25 and 7-Eleven stores accept foreign cards (look for the Global ATM sign).

🌿 Climate Card (기후동행카드) Best for Long Stays

기후동행카드 — Seoul's unlimited transit pass

The Climate Card (기후동행카드) is Seoul's unlimited public transit pass — covers all subway lines, city buses, and even Ttukseom & Hangang ferry boats within Seoul. If you're spending 5+ days in Seoul and plan to move around a lot, it can be better value than paying per ride with T-money.

Options: 30-day pass (~₩65,000) or 5-day pass (~₩20,000 — check current pricing at the time of travel). Buy at major subway station machines or at convenience stores. Requires a separate card (not the same as T-money). Note: does not cover the AREX airport express or intercity buses — only Seoul city transit. Also does not cover Gyeonggi/Incheon metro lines that extend outside Seoul city limits.

💡 Quick math: A single subway ride costs ~₩1,400–₩1,600. If you take more than ~13–14 rides in 5 days, the 5-day Climate Card pays off. For a week of active sightseeing across Seoul, it almost certainly does.

📶 WiFi Egg & Connectivity

공공 와이파이 & 포켓 와이파이 에그

Pocket WiFi Egg: Rent at Incheon Airport arrivals hall — KT, SKT, or LG U+ counters before you exit customs. ~₩8,000–₩11,000/day. One device shares with multiple people. Pre-book online and collect at airport. Highly recommended.

Public WiFi: Free "PublicWifi@Seoul" network throughout subways, parks, and tourist areas. Reliable indoors, patchy outdoors — don't depend on it alone.

SIM Card: Same airport counters. Tourist SIMs with unlimited data for 5–30 days. Requires passport. Works in most unlocked phones.

03

Getting Around Seoul 지하철 & 버스

🚇 Express vs. Local Subway Important!

급행 vs. 완행 — Know the difference

Many Seoul subway lines have two types of trains: Express (급행) — skips most intermediate stations and is dramatically faster — and Local (완행) — stops everywhere. The type is shown on platform digital boards and announced before arrival.

Line 9 is the most famous for this — the express skips about two-thirds of stations from the airport side to Gangnam. If an express is 3 minutes away vs a local now, it's almost always worth waiting.

💡 App tip: Download Naver Map or Kakao Map — both excellent in English, show real-time trains, and indicate express vs. local. Google Maps also works well for Seoul transit routing.
LineColorKey StopsNotes
Line 2🟢 GreenCOEX (Samseong), Hongdae, SinchonCircular — very useful
Line 3🟠 OrangeGyeongbokgung, Anguk (Bukchon)Best for palace area
Line 9🟡 GoldAirport ↔ Gangnam ↔ Express TerminalHas express trains — very fast
AREX🔵 BlueIncheon Airport → Seoul StationAirport express only
Bundang Line🟡 YellowSeolleung, Sunae, PangyoGangnam south corridor
04

The COEX Area 삼성동 코엑스

🗺️ COEX B1 Official Underground Map

Samseong Station (Line 2) enters bottom-left · Bongeunsa Station (Line 9) enters bottom-right · All buildings connect at B1

COEX B1 Underground Map
MallVeg Options★ 4.3

Starfield COEX Mall

코엑스몰 — 513 Yeongdong-daero, Gangnam · Line 2, Samseong Station

Seoul's massive underground mall in the heart of Gangnam. Connected to the subway directly. Has the Star Library, Baskin Robbins Brown (100 flavors), MegaBox boutique cinemas, dozens of restaurants, and a vegetarian-friendly food hall. Hours: 10:30am–10pm daily.

🕐 Daily 10:30am – 10:00pm
Starfield Library 별마당 도서관
Free Entry★ 4.6

⭐ Starfield Library

별마당 도서관 — Inside COEX Mall B1

A jaw-dropping open "library" with floor-to-ceiling bookshelves stretching multiple stories. More of a stunning architectural moment than a working library — absolutely worth walking through. Free for all. Starbucks inside. Gets crowded on weekends so go early morning for the best photos. One of Seoul's most Instagrammed spots.

🕐 Daily 10:30am – 10:00pm · Free
Hyundai Dept Store — B1 Food Hall
ShoppingFood Hall★ 4.3

Hyundai Department Store

현대백화점 무역센터점 — 517 Teheran-ro

Connected to COEX via underground walkway — no need to go outside. 10 floors of fashion plus an excellent B1–B2 food hall with Korean banchan, baked goods, salads, and prepared foods. After 6pm, discounted pricing on many food items — great for grabbing Korean snacks and desserts at a deal. Sky garden on the top floor.

🕐 Mon–Thu 10:30am–8pm · Fri–Sun 10:30am–8:30pm
BR Brown — 100 Flavors
Vegetarian★ 4.3

🍦 Baskin Robbins Brown — 100 Flavors

배스킨라빈스 파르나스몰점 — Parnas Mall (connected to Hyundai/COEX)

An upscale Baskin Robbins concept store inside the Parnas Mall (the connected building next to Hyundai and COEX). About 100 rotating flavors — unique Korean options like green tea red bean, black sesame, rice cake, sweet potato, and persimmon. Order from a touch kiosk in English. Far better than any Baskin Robbins you've seen before.

🕐 Daily 8am–10pm (Sat–Sun from 9am)
MegaBox Boutique M — 부티크 영화관
Cinema★ 4.4

🎬 MegaBox COEX — Boutique Cinemas

메가박스 코엑스 · 부티크 영화관 — 524 Bongeunsa-ro

Korean cinemas are a step above most. MegaBox has "Boutique M" — private luxury theater rooms with recliners and couples' seats. Even the regular screens have excellent sound systems. Korean popcorn flavors: shrimp, honey butter, corn cheese, caramel, and more. Grab popcorn even if you skip the movie. Open until 3am.

🕐 Daily 9am – 3am
JW Marriott Seoul
Luxury HotelVeg Options★ 4.4

🏨 JW Marriott Seoul

JW 메리어트 서울 — 176 Sinbanpo-ro, Seocho · Connected to Shinsegae Dept Store · ~15 min taxi from COEX

One of Seoul's premier luxury hotels with exceptional dining and amenities. The hotel is directly connected to Shinsegae Department Store for convenient shopping access. Multiple restaurants inside ranging from Korean fine dining to international cuisine, all with good vegetarian options on request. The lobby lounge is a beautiful spot for afternoon tea or cocktails.

Great option for a special dinner or a treat — worth the taxi from COEX. Also accessible via Airport Limousine Bus No. 6020 direct from Incheon Airport.

🕐 Hotel 24hr · Restaurants vary · +82 2-6282-6262
Bongeunsa Temple & Surrounding Area
봉은사 — Bongeunsa Temple
Free EntryTemple Food★ 4.6

🛕 Bongeunsa Temple

봉은사 — 531 Bongeunsa-ro · 3 min walk from COEX

A 1,200-year-old Buddhist temple (founded 794 AD) sitting peacefully across the road from COEX's glass towers — the contrast is surreal and beautiful. Free to enter. Multiple prayer halls, a massive standing Mireuk Buddha statue, stone lanterns, and resident monks. Visit at dusk when lanterns glow. The adjacent cemetery garden (묘) is a historically significant and serene space worth a quiet walk through.

🕐 Daily 5:00am – 10:00pm · Free

☕ Café Street near Bongeunsa & Seolleung

선릉 카페 거리 — The famous café street is around Seolleung Station, 10 min from COEX

The famous café street in this area runs around Seolleung Station (선릉역) — a dense cluster of independent specialty coffee shops, wine bars, and small restaurants just outside the royal tomb park. The streets around Bongeunsa also have some cafés worth stopping at after temple visiting, but for the real café street experience, head toward Seolleung.

선릉 — Seolleung Royal Tombs
₩1,000 EntryUNESCO★ 4.6

🌿 Seolleung & Jeongneung Royal Tombs

선릉과 정릉 — 1 Seolleung-ro 100-gil · Line 2, Seolleung Station Exit 8 · 10 min walk from COEX

A peaceful UNESCO World Heritage royal tomb park tucked inside Gangnam — a surreal green sanctuary amid office towers. The tombs of Joseon kings Seongjong and Jungjong sit on beautifully maintained grass mounds shaded by ancient trees. Entry only ₩1,000. Allow 30–90 minutes.

The famous café street (선릉역 카페 거리) runs right outside — independent specialty coffee shops, wine bars, and small restaurants. Much calmer and more local than the COEX zone.

🕐 Tue–Sun 6am–9pm · Closed Monday · Entry ₩1,000
Food Near COEX
삼원가든 — Samwon Garden
Korean BBQ★ 4.1

🥩 Samwon Garden

삼원가든 — 835 Eonju-ro · ~15 min walk from COEX

One of Seoul's oldest and most famous Korean BBQ restaurants, open since 1976. Garden setting with trees and a small waterfall inside. Exceptional galbi (beef short ribs) and bulgogi. Slightly upscale — this is a special meal, not a quick bite. Recently renovated interior. Reservation recommended on weekends. The garden atmosphere is genuinely beautiful.

🕐 Mon–Fri 11:30am–2:30pm, 5:30–9:30pm · Sat–Sun 11am–9pm
Plantude — 플랜튜드
Fully VeganInside COEX★ 4.5

🌱 Plantude

플랜튜드 — COEX Mall B1, I-111 · Right inside COEX near Starfield Library

The only fully vegan restaurant inside COEX Mall. Hidden on B1 near the bottom level of the Starfield Library (head left from library escalators, walk north). Vegan ramen, pasta, eggplant curry, tofu shakshuka, mandu, and vegan ice cream and tiramisu. English menu. Friendly staff, clean space.

Finding it: Escalator down to B1 at the Starfield Library, stay on B1, head left (north) — you'll see it on your left.

🕐 Daily 11:00am – 8:00pm
Workshop by Baskin Robbins
Vegetarian★ 4.3

🔬 Workshop by Baskin Robbins

워크샵 바이 배스킨라빈스 — Gangnam · ~15 min taxi south of COEX

A completely different concept from the BR Brown store inside COEX — Workshop by Baskin Robbins is a custom dessert experience where you can order unique sculpted ice cream creations in the shape of cute characters, animals, and seasonal designs. The minimalist interior feels more like a design studio than an ice cream shop. Far more experiential and creative than a standard BR. Worth the short taxi if you want something truly unique and photo-worthy.

Dunkin' Wonders — 청담
Vegetarian Options★ 4.3

🍩 Dunkin' Wonders — Cheongdam

던킨 원더스 청담 — 329 Dosan-daero, Cheongdam · ~10 min taxi from COEX

Korea's Dunkin' is a completely different experience from the US, and the Cheongdam Wonders flagship takes it to another level — a beautifully designed 2-floor café with unique Korean flavors like sweet potato, red bean, green tea, and honey rice cake. The donuts here are stunningly crafted and look almost too pretty to eat. Spacious seating with chargers on both floors. In the heart of Cheongdam's luxury street.

05

Han River Parks 한강공원

Starbucks Mangwon — 한강뷰
Café★ 4.4

☕ Starbucks Mangwon Hangang Park

스타벅스 망원한강공원점 — 435 Maponaru-gil · Line 6, Mangwon Station

A beloved Starbucks right on the Han River in the Mangwon neighborhood — large, spacious, with incredible river views through floor-to-ceiling windows. Very popular on weekends so arrive early or on a weekday for a seat. The Mangwon neighborhood around it is full of independent, beautifully designed cafés as well — a great half-day exploration zone. The weekend Mangwon Market is also nearby.

🕐 Daily 9:00am – 9:00pm
Nike Seoul — 나이키 서울
ShoppingCustomization★ 4.7

👟 Nike Seoul — Hongdae Flagship

나이키 홍대 — 23 Hongik-ro, Mapo-gu · Line 2, Hongdae Entrance Station Exit 9

Nike's flagship Seoul store in Hongdae is one of the most unique Nike locations in the world. The highlight is the customization studio — you can design and print your own Nike t-shirt, hoodie, or tote bag with custom graphics, text, and Seoul-themed prints on the spot. Staff speak English well and the process is fun and fast.

6 floors of exclusive Seoul-edition sneakers and apparel, many colorways only available here. The store itself is beautifully designed — worth visiting even just to browse. Book the customization studio in advance as slots fill up, especially on weekends.

🕐 Daily 11:00am – 10:00pm · +82 2-334-6593
06

Bukchon & Palaces 북촌 & 궁

경복궁 — Gyeongbokgung Palace
Palace★ 4.6

🏯 Gyeongbokgung Palace

경복궁 — 161 Sajik-ro · Line 3, Gyeongbokgung Station Exit 5

The grandest of Seoul's five palaces, built in 1395. Massive complex — allow 2–3 hours. Changing of the Guard ceremony at 10am and 2pm (check schedule, closed Tuesdays). Entry ₩3,000. Rent a hanbok (traditional dress) from nearby shops and enter free. The National Folk Museum is inside the grounds — also free with entry. Arrive before 9am to beat the crowds.

🕐 Wed–Mon 9am–5pm (6pm summer) · Closed Tuesday
북촌한옥마을 — Bukchon Hanok Village
Free to Walk★ 4.4

🏘️ Bukchon Hanok Village

북촌한옥마을 — Gyedong-gil · Line 3, Anguk Station Exit 2

A neighborhood of preserved traditional Korean wooden houses (hanok). The view from the top of Bukchon-ro 11-gil looking downhill over tiled rooftops is one of the most photographed images in Korea. Go before 9am to avoid crowds and be quiet — real families live here. Small cafés, galleries, and perfume shops dot the alleys. Allow 2 hours to wander. Visitor hours: 10am–5pm in some zones.

창덕궁 — Changdeokgung Secret Garden
UNESCO Palace★ 4.6

🏯 Changdeokgung Palace

창덕궁 — 99 Yulgok-ro · Line 3, Anguk Station Exit 3

UNESCO World Heritage Site — more intimate and organically designed than Gyeongbokgung, following the natural landscape. The Secret Garden (후원) at the back is stunning — 78 acres of pavilions, ponds, and ancient trees once reserved for royalty. Requires a separate guided tour (English tours available — book ahead at the website). Entry: ₩3,000 palace / ₩8,000 with Secret Garden. Closed Mondays.

🕐 Tue–Sun 9am–6pm · Closed Monday
부빙 — Boobing Bingsu
VegetarianDessert★ 4.5

🍧 Boobing — Famous Bingsu

부빙 가회점 — 3-4 Bukchon-ro 7-gil, Jongno · 5 min walk from Bukchon

One of Seoul's most talked-about bingsu spots, tucked in an alley right in Bukchon. Portions are absurdly generous — easily enough for 3–4 people per bowl. Flavors: black sesame, strawberry, hojicha (red bean and rice balls inside), corn, fig, and seasonal specials. Fine-shaved ice that melts perfectly.

Gets busy — arrive early or expect a wait. Closed Mondays.

🕐 Tue–Sun 1:00pm – 6:00pm · Closed Monday
💡 Palace Pass: The integrated ticket (통합관람권, ₩10,000) covers all four main palaces — Gyeongbokgung, Changdeokgung, Changgyeonggung, Deoksugung — plus Jongmyo Shrine. Great value if you plan to visit multiple in a day.
Famous Food & Street Area Next to Changdeokgung
07

Day Trip: Daejeon & KAIST 대전 · 50분

대전역 — Daejeon Station
KTX Train

🚄 Daejeon Station

대전역 — 50 min from Seoul by KTX · ~₩23,700 one way

Take the KTX high-speed train from Seoul Station — about 50 minutes. Buy tickets at korail.go.kr (English) or the Korail Talk app, or at Seoul Station ticket machines (press English on screen). Book in advance on weekends. From Daejeon Station: taxi to KAIST is ~₩8,000 (15 min), or Bus 104. KAIST campus is open to walk through.

성심당 — Sungsimdang Bakery
Mostly Vegetarian★ 4.5

🥐 Sungsimdang Bakery

성심당 — 15 Daejong-ro 480beon-gil · 10 min walk from Daejeon Station

Famous enough to be a reason people travel to Daejeon specifically. Open since 1956. The signature item is 튀김소보로 (fried cream soborro pastry) — arrives warm and sells out fast. Also try 판타롱 부추빵 (chive bread). Arrive early or expect a 30–60 minute line. Grab a tray and tongs — the bread selection is enormous. Most baked goods are naturally vegetarian. Hours: 8am–10pm daily.

🚄 KTX booking steps: (1) Go to korail.go.kr → click English → Reservation. (2) From: Seoul (서울), To: Daejeon (대전). (3) Select KTX. (4) Choose standard seat (일반실). (5) Pay by card. Print or screenshot your ticket. Bring passport for the foreigner rate option.
08

Jeju Island 제주도

✈️ Getting to Jeju: Fly from Gimpo Airport (Seoul, ~1hr) or Incheon (~1hr 10min). Korean Air, Asiana, Jeju Air, T'way, Jin Air all fly frequently. Book via Naver Flight or airline apps. Getting around Jeju: rent a car — public transit is very limited outside Jeju City. International driving license required (check your country's IDP rules). KakaoTaxi works too for shorter trips.
한라산 — Hallasan Crater Lake
Free EntryHiking★ 4.7

🌋 Hallasan Mountain

한라산 — Center of Jeju Island · National Park

Korea's highest peak — a dormant volcano rising to 1,947m at Jeju's center. Multiple trails: Seongpanak (13.9km, reaches summit) and Yeongsil (shorter, scenic) are most popular. Summit requires early start and registration in advance. Full hike takes 7–9 hours. Bring layers — summit is much colder. No overnight camping. Bring plenty of water and food. Absolutely worth it.

🕐 Daily 5am–6pm · Free
성산일출봉 — Seongsan Ilchulbong
UNESCO★ 4.7

🌅 Seongsan Ilchulbong

성산일출봉 — Seogwipo, East Jeju · UNESCO World Heritage

The iconic "Sunrise Peak" — a dramatic volcanic tuff cone rising straight from the ocean with a massive grassy crater at the summit. The 20–30 minute climb is well-paved with steps. Entry ₩2,000. Stunning at sunrise (the name means "sunrise") but beautiful any time of day. The haenyeo (female divers) do a traditional diving show at the base at 2pm daily. One of Jeju's absolute must-sees.

🕐 Daily (seasonal hours) · Entry ₩2,000
함덕해수욕장 — Hamdeok Beach
FreeBeach★ 4.5

🏖️ Hamdeok Beach

함덕해수욕장 — Jocheon-eup, Jeju City side

One of Jeju's most beautiful beaches — strikingly clear blue-green shallow water against white sand and green coastal mountains. Family-friendly and calm. Small cafés, restaurants, and convenience stores line the waterfront. Great for swimming in summer, beautiful scenic walks any season. The western coast (Aewol, Hyeopjae) also has the famous "Jeju Café Road" — Instagram-famous ocean-view cafés lining the coastal drive.

만장굴 — Manjanggul Lava Tube
UNESCO★ 4.2

🌊 Manjanggul Lava Tube

만장굴 — Gujwa-eup, East Jeju · UNESCO World Heritage

One of the world's longest lava tube cave systems — you walk 1km through an other-worldly underground tunnel formed by ancient volcanic flows. Temperature inside is a cool 18°C regardless of outside weather (great in summer heat). Bring a light jacket. The cave floor is rough volcanic rock — wear proper shoes. Entry ₩4,000. Budget about 1 hour. The local restaurant at the entrance is excellent.

🕐 Entry ₩4,000 · ~1 hr walk
돌하르방 — Dolharubang
Cultural IconFree★ 4.0

🗿 Dolharubang — Stone Grandfathers

돌하르방 — Found across Jeju Island · Bukchon Dol Hareubang Park has the largest collection

The dolharubang (돌하르방, meaning "stone grandfather") are Jeju's most iconic symbols — large volcanic basalt statues with wide eyes, flat noses, and their hands folded over their bellies. They were originally placed at the entrances of fortress gates to ward off evil spirits.

The famous legend: Women who rub the nose of a dolharubang are said to become pregnant with a son (아들을 낳는다). The noses on many statues across Jeju are noticeably worn smooth from centuries of hopeful rubbing. Whether you believe it or not — it's a fun thing to do and part of the Jeju experience! You'll find dolharubang statues all over the island, in front of temples, parks, and tourist sites.

🕐 Bukchon Dol Hareubang Park: Mon–Fri 9am–6pm, Sat 10am–4pm · Closed Sunday · Statues across Jeju are free and always accessible

🍊 Jeju Local Produce — Must Try

제주 로컬 특산물

Hallabong (한라봉) — Jeju's famous sweet mandarin citrus, absolutely delicious and unique to the island. Green tangerine (청귤) products — juice, tea, chocolate, candy. Black sesame and Jeju barley snacks. The 5-day traditional markets (오일장) around Jeju are full of fresh vegetables, citrus, and local snacks — all great for the vegetarian. The non-vegetarian friend should try Jeju black pork (흑돼지) — a unique local specialty.

Seoul & Jeju Travel Guide · Made with care for two friends, one vegetarian 🌱
All prices in Korean Won (₩) · 1 USD ≈ ₩1,350 · Always carry some cash · Enjoy!
